source: Invest Like the Best — The cyclone under the surface (EP.260)
summary: Internet advertising and e-commerce companies face a double headwind of COVID hangover and economic slowdown; search is the exception
summary_extended: Baker argues that internet advertising and e-commerce companies face a genuine double headwind in early 2022: first, a COVID normalization effect where demand that was pulled forward during the pandemic is now reverting below trend, and second, GDP sensitivity that becomes a drag as the broader economy slows. He supports this view with 'reasonable telemetry' from app downloads, web traffic, and credit card data, all pointing to fundamental weakness across most internet names. Search is the key exception to this bearish view because it is levered to the resurgence in services spending — as consumers shift back to travel, dining, and experiences, search query volume and monetization benefit. Subscription-based internet companies are also more resilient, behaving more like software in their revenue predictability. The investment implication is to be cautious on ad-dependent and e-commerce platforms broadly, while favoring search-exposed names as a relative safe haven within the internet sector.
detail: Baker expects broad fundamental weakness in internet names (e-commerce and advertising) due to COVID normalization pulling demand back below trend and GDP sensitivity in a slowing economy. Search is the exception because it is levered to the resurgence in services spending. Subscription internet companies are more similar to software in their resilience.
confidence_evidence: Baker references 'reasonable telemetry' from app downloads, web traffic, and credit card data, and explicitly distinguishes search from the rest.
quote_fragment: real legitimate fundamental weakness in almost all of the internet names, maybe with the exception of search

ticker_audit.reasoning: Baker explicitly calls out search as the exception to broad internet weakness, and Alphabet/Google is the dominant US-listed search advertising platform — the clearest implied beneficiary of the search-as-exception thesis.
